metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct AC5 to state 'AGENT_TIER (not CODEBASE_GROUNDED)' so the registration test checks the right registry set, removing the contradiction with the Scope section.
findings.finding: AC5 labels G7 as 'an AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion' for the purpose of the registration test, but the Scope section explicitly says G7 is NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED — it belongs only in AGENT_TIER (with a parenthetical explanation of why). An executing agent following AC5 literally would write a test asserting G7 appears in CODEBASE_GROUNDED, which would either fail (if the executor correctly uses AGENT_TIER) or produce a mis-registered criterion that silently grants it the wrong capabilities.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5 ('a test confirms G7 is registered as an AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion'); Scope section — 'register G7 in CANONICAL_LLM + AGENT_TIER (NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED — that set is for criteria whose VERIFY re-grounds against CODE)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC5's clause 'a test confirms G7 is registered as an AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion (so it gets the tool)' with 'a test confirms G7 is registered in CANONICAL_LLM and in AGENT_TIER (not CODEBASE_GROUNDED) — which is the mechanism by which the pydantic_ai runner grants the show_ticket tool — and the rubric file references reading the parent ticket via show_ticket.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the contradiction between AC4 ('AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED') and Scope/What ('AGENT_TIER, NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED') by correcting AC4 to reference AGENT_TIER only, consistent with the plan's explicit rationale.
findings.finding: AC4 says 'a test confirms G7 is registered as an AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion (so it gets the tool)' but the Scope section explicitly says 'register G7 in CANONICAL_LLM + AGENT_TIER (NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED)' and the What section says 'exec: AGENT'. An implementer following the Scope/What would register G7 in AGENT_TIER only; an implementer following AC4 would also add it to CODEBASE_GROUNDED. These directly contradict each other.
findings.location: AC4 vs. Scope (registry.py paragraph) and What (exec: AGENT bullet)
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC4's test assertion to: 'a test confirms G7 is registered as an AGENT_TIER criterion (so it gets the show_ticket tool) and is NOT in CODEBASE_GROUNDED.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the CODEBASE_GROUNDED contradiction: AC5 must be corrected to 'AGENT_TIER' only (not CODEBASE_GROUNDED), consistent with the Scope section's explicit rationale and the registry.py:62-65 comment that tool access is granted by AGENT_TIER membership, not CODEBASE_GROUNDED.
findings.finding: AC5 contradicts the Scope section on CODEBASE_GROUNDED membership: Scope explicitly says 'NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED', but AC5 says 'a test confirms G7 is registered as an AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion'. CODEBASE_GROUNDED is `frozenset({"E4", "G1G2", "A1", "G6"})` (registry.py:66) and is what `plan_review_grounding` uses to decide whether to run the expensive agentic verifier (workflow_ops.py:229-230). If G7 is added to CODEBASE_GROUNDED, every G7 finding will trigger the agentic verifier even though G7 grounds against the ticket store, not code — and the Scope section correctly notes it should NOT be in CODEBASE_GROUNDED for exactly this reason.
findings.location: Scope section ('register G7 in CANONICAL_LLM + AGENT_TIER (NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED)') vs. AC5 ('a test confirms G7 is registered as an AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion')
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC5 from 'G7 is registered as an AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion' to 'G7 is registered in CANONICAL_LLM and AGENT_TIER (but NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED), so it receives the show_ticket tool via its AGENT exec mode without triggering the agentic verifier for its findings.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Make the plan-review.yaml G7 batch entry mandatory (not conditional): add `- prompt: plan-review-G7 / when: '${{ steps.assemble.outputs.include_G7 }}'` to the `criteria:` list in plan-review.yaml — the criteria list IS enumerated, so the entry is required for G7 to run.
findings.finding: The plan states 'add G7 to the static `criteria:` batch list IF criteria are enumerated there', framing it as conditional. The criteria ARE enumerated in plan-review.yaml (lines 55-117): every criterion has a static `- prompt: plan-review-<ID> / when: ${{...}}` entry. Without a G7 entry in this YAML, the `plan_review_assemble_criteria` op will emit an `include_G7` boolean but the batch step has no slot to read it — G7 will never run even when `route_criteria()` includes it.
findings.location: Scope section: 'src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml (add G7 to the static `criteria:` batch list if criteria are enumerated there)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the Scope bullet to: 'src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml — add `- prompt: plan-review-G7 / when: \'${{ steps.assemble.outputs.include_G7 }}\'` to the static `criteria:` batch list (confirmed enumerated at lines 55-117).' Remove the 'if criteria are enumerated there' hedge.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct AC #4 to say 'registered as an AGENT_TIER criterion' (not CODEBASE_GROUNDED) so the test assertion matches the plan body's explicit instruction and does not inadvertently add G7 to the CODEBASE_GROUNDED set.
findings.finding: AC #4 contradicts the plan body on CODEBASE_GROUNDED membership. AC #4 states 'a test confirms G7 is registered as an AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion (so it gets the tool),' but the plan body explicitly says G7 should NOT be in CODEBASE_GROUNDED ('NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED — that set is for criteria whose VERIFY re-grounds against CODE; G7 grounds against the parent TICKET via the store'). CODEBASE_GROUNDED membership also controls whether `plan_review_grounding` in workflow_ops.py triggers the agentic verifier for G7 findings — so the AC wording, if followed literally, would add G7 to CODEBASE_GROUNDED and cause the agentic verifier to run for every G7 finding.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #4 vs. Scope section ('NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED' note)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion' in AC #4 with 'AGENT_TIER criterion (not CODEBASE_GROUNDED)' to match the Scope section's explicit parenthetical.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove the 'if criteria are enumerated there' hedge from the plan and treat the `plan-review.yaml` `criteria:` entry for G7 as a required (not conditional) edit.
findings.finding: The plan's Scope says 'add G7 to the static `criteria:` batch list **if criteria are enumerated there**' — but inspection of `plan-review.yaml` confirms criteria ARE statically enumerated (lines 55–117 list 30+ prompt entries each with a `when:` guard). The conditional 'if' makes this an optional edit in the plan text, but it is actually a required edit. Missing this entry means G7's `include_G7` output from `plan_review_assemble_criteria` has no corresponding `when:` slot in the batch, so the criterion will never fire in the v3 workflow engine despite routing correctly.
findings.location: Scope section: 'src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml (add G7 to the static criteria: batch list if criteria are enumerated there)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC item 4 with the What section: the test should confirm G7 is in `AGENT_TIER` (for tool access) and `CANONICAL_LLM` but NOT in `CODEBASE_GROUNDED` (which would trigger the costly agentic verifier path via `plan_review_grounding`); update AC#4 to read 'a test confirms G7 is registered in AGENT_TIER and CANONICAL_LLM (not CODEBASE_GROUNDED) and that its rubric references reading the parent ticket.'
findings.finding: AC item 4 says 'a test confirms G7 is registered as an AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion (so it gets the tool)' — contradicting the What section which explicitly says 'NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED.' Adding G7 to CODEBASE_GROUNDED is mechanically wrong: (a) tool access for AGENT-tier criteria comes from AGENT_TIER membership alone, not CODEBASE_GROUNDED (registry.py:62-65 comment confirms this); (b) the plan_review_grounding workflow op (workflow_ops.py:229-232) triggers the expensive AGENTIC verifier whenever any finding cites a CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion — adding G7 there forces agentic verification on every leaf-with-parent G7 finding, a cost amplification the plan does not intend; (c) CODEBASE_GROUNDED is explicitly for criteria 'whose job is to reason about the live codebase' (registry.py:60-66) — G7 reasons against the ticket store, not code.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria item 4 vs What section (AGENT_TIER/CODEBASE_GROUNDED paragraph)
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC#4 to: 'a test confirms G7 is in AGENT_TIER and CANONICAL_LLM but NOT in CODEBASE_GROUNDED (tool access comes from AGENT_TIER; CODEBASE_GROUNDED triggers the agentic verifier, which is not needed for a ticket-store-grounded criterion), and that its rubric file references show_ticket for reading the parent.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix AC #4 to say 'a test confirms G7 is in AGENT_TIER (not CODEBASE_GROUNDED)' — the tool is granted by AGENT_TIER membership / execution_mode:agentic, not by CODEBASE_GROUNDED, per the registry.py:62-65 comment and the Scope section.
findings.finding: AC #4 contradicts the Scope section on CODEBASE_GROUNDED membership: the AC says 'a test confirms G7 is registered as an AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion (so it gets the tool)' but the Scope section explicitly states 'NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED'. The codebase confirms CODEBASE_GROUNDED = frozenset({"E4","G1G2","A1","G6"}) and its doc comment (registry.py:62-65) explains CODEBASE_GROUNDED is a SEMANTIC set (whose Pass-2 verification re-grounds against CODE), not a tool-capability boundary. An implementer writing the AC-driven test would assert G7 ∈ CODEBASE_GROUNDED — which is wrong — while the Scope section says the opposite. The correct test is G7 ∈ AGENT_TIER.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #4 vs Scope section ('NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED — that set is for criteria whose VERIFY re-grounds against CODE')
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion' in AC #4 with 'AGENT_TIER criterion (not CODEBASE_GROUNDED)' and confirm the test asserts `'G7' in registry.AGENT_TIER and 'G7' not in registry.CODEBASE_GROUNDED`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC4's test oracle: replace 'AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion' with 'AGENT_TIER criterion (not CODEBASE_GROUNDED)' to match the Scope's explicit NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED directive, ensuring the test checks the correct registry set.
findings.finding: AC4's test oracle is self-contradictory: it directs a test to confirm G7 is registered as an 'AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion' so it gets the tool, but the Scope section explicitly states G7 must be in AGENT_TIER 'NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED.' A test author following AC4 literally could check CODEBASE_GROUNDED membership (wrong tier) and either lock in incorrect registration or write a tautologically-passing test against the wrong oracle — the classic self-authored-oracle anti-pattern.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4; cross-ref: Scope ('register G7 in CANONICAL_LLM + AGENT_TIER (NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED)')
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC4 to read: 'a test confirms G7 is registered in CANONICAL_LLM and AGENT_TIER (and NOT in CODEBASE_GROUNDED) — verifying it receives the show_ticket tool via execution_mode, not via the codebase-grounded tool grant — and the rubric file references reading the parent ticket via show_ticket.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC4 with the Scope section: if G7 belongs to AGENT_TIER (not CODEBASE_GROUNDED), update AC4 to say 'a test confirms G7 is registered as an AGENT_TIER criterion (not CODEBASE_GROUNDED) and that its rubric references reading the parent ticket via show_ticket'.
findings.finding: AC4 states 'a test confirms G7 is registered as an AGENT/CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion (so it gets the tool)' — but the Scope section explicitly states 'register G7 in CANONICAL_LLM + AGENT_TIER (NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED — that set is for criteria whose VERIFY re-grounds against CODE; G7 grounds against the parent TICKET via the store)'. These two statements directly contradict each other; an implementer writing the test will not know which tier membership to assert.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC4 vs. Scope section (registry.py registration note)
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC4's parenthetical from '(so it gets the tool)' to align with the Scope's explicit NOT CODEBASE_GROUNDED rationale: 'a test confirms G7 is registered in CANONICAL_LLM and AGENT_TIER (not CODEBASE_GROUNDED — tool access is granted by AGENT_TIER membership per registry.py:62-65) and the rubric references reading the parent ticket.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State in AC4 (or in the What section's promotion-path description) the target format/language for the fire-rate query (e.g., 'a jq command against the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar JSON') and name the sidecar field(s) that must appear as anchors in the grep check.
findings.finding: AC4 requires the committed `g7-promotion-path.md` file to contain 'the exact query to compute G7's fire-rate + validity from the REVIEW_RESULT sidecars' but specifies no query language, format, or schema anchor for the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar. The grep-based CI check verifies presence of '0.6', '0.7', and the recording location, but does not verify that the query is actually executable or targets the correct sidecar field. An executor who writes a prose description of the query (rather than a runnable command) would pass the grep check but leave the fire-rate observability path broken.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 ('the exact query to compute G7's fire-rate + validity from the REVIEW_RESULT sidecars'); What section — promotion-path.md description

findings.finding: The plan asserts 'the parent_id is already available to the criterion (the ticket-graph blob / context already carries parent linkage via pass1._ticket_graph_blob)' — but per the codebase, _ticket_graph_blob is injected ONLY for the ISF single-turn criterion (pass1.py:478-488), never into AGENT-tier criteria. AGENT criteria (G1G2, E4, A1, G6, and the proposed G7) receive only plan_text + rubric + extra_context via pass1_chunk; the parent_id from ctx.state is absent from their context window. If the G7 rubric instructs the agent to 'call show_ticket(<parent_id>)', the agent has no reliable mechanism to know what parent_id to supply — the plan text (title+description field) does not contain it. The mechanism is incomplete as described.
findings.location: What section — 'CONTEXT ASSEMBLY — NO new context plumbing'; also Scope section and AC line 5
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section and the rubric design, replace the claim that the ticket-graph blob supplies parent_id with an explicit injection: when G7 is routed for a ticket, extract `ctx.state.get('parent_id')` in `run_pass1` and pass it as `extra_context` to G7's `_chunk` call (the same pattern as `decomp_context` for G5). The rubric then says 'the parent_id is provided above; call show_ticket(<parent_id>) to read the parent plan.' This is a minimal, targeted context-plumbing addition that the no-new-plumbing rationale should acknowledge as a narrow exception.

findings.finding: The plan claims '`validate_packaged_routing()` and the applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis' — but the existing `_validate_routing_entry` at overlay.py:190-212 is already permissive: it checks for legacy keys (`levels`, `container_only`) and validates `scope` values, but has NO allowlist on `applies_at` keys. A `require_parent_id: true` entry is silently accepted today without any code change. The plan's scope says 'the applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis' — leaving ambiguous whether active value-validation of `require_parent_id` (e.g., must be a boolean) is intended. If the plan means only that the validator won't reject it, no code change is needed; if it means the validator should actively check the value, a code change to overlay.py is needed but that file is absent from the Scope.
findings.location: What section — 'ROUTING' paragraph; Scope section; overlay.py:190-212

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the new coach move fully: assign it an id (e.g. '13'), a `name` ('realign-to-parent'), a `template` with `{subject}` (e.g. 'Realign {subject} to the parent ticket's declared scope, or update the parent first if the parent is wrong.'), and an `applies_when: ["G7"]` list.
findings.finding: The new 'parent-realign' coach move has no id, name, template string, or applies_when list specified. The plan says 'the coach MOVE_REGISTRY (one parent-realign move in passes.py)' but the existing MOVE_REGISTRY schema (coach.py:35-58, validated by validate_move_registry) requires `name`, `template` with a `{subject}` placeholder, and optionally `applies_when`. Without these, an implementer cannot write the move entry or its validation test, and `validate_move_registry(strict=True)` will raise at load time.
findings.location: Scope section: 'the coach MOVE_REGISTRY (one parent-realign move in passes.py)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to MOVE_REGISTRY: `"13": {"name": "realign-to-parent", "template": "Realign {subject} to the parent ticket's declared scope; if the parent is wrong, update the parent first (which stales its own review attestation) and then re-review this leaf.", "applies_when": ["G7"]}`.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `_validate_routing_entry` in `criteria/overlay.py` needs a code change to validate `require_parent_id` (type-check it as boolean), and if so, add `criteria/overlay.py` to the Scope section's edit targets.
findings.finding: The plan names `_validate_routing_entry` (in `criteria/overlay.py`) and `validate_packaged_routing()` (in `registry.py`) as needing to 'accept the new axis' — but the current `_validate_routing_entry` silently ignores unknown `applies_at` keys (no allowlist enforcement). If the intent is to add positive schema validation of `require_parent_id`, `criteria/overlay.py` is an edit target that is not explicitly listed in the Scope section. If the intent is merely that the validator not reject it (silent passthrough), no code change is needed — but the plan's wording is ambiguous.
findings.location: Scope section: 'validate_packaged_routing() and the applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis'; criteria/overlay.py:_validate_routing_entry:190-212

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add error-handling guidance to the G7 rubric prompt and/or the AGENT runner integration: specify that if `show_ticket(parent_id)` returns an error or empty result, the criterion should emit a single low-severity advisory finding (e.g. 'parent ticket not readable — containment check skipped') rather than crashing or silently passing.
findings.finding: The plan adds a runtime `show_ticket(parent_id)` call inside an AGENT criterion but specifies no error-handling or graceful-degradation path for the case where that call fails (parent ticket deleted, store unreachable, access-control rejection, or `parent_id` present in routing metadata but not resolvable). The routing gate confirms `has_parent_id` is true but cannot guarantee the ticket is fetchable at execution time. The rubric prompt and the criterion's registered behavior are both silent on this failure mode.
findings.location: What section — CONTEXT ASSEMBLY paragraph; Scope — 'a new reviewers/plan_review_G7.md rubric prompt'; AC line: 'G7's rubric instructs the AGENT to read the parent … via its show_ticket tool'
findings.suggested_fix: In `plan_review_G7.md`, add a section: 'If `show_ticket(<parent_id>)` returns an error or empty body, emit one advisory finding: "G7: parent ticket <parent_id> could not be read; containment check skipped. Verify the parent ticket is accessible." and return no other findings.' This keeps the review step alive while making the gap visible to the operator.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a graceful-degradation clause to the G7 rubric: when `show_ticket(parent_id)` returns an error string (starts with 'Error:'), the agent should abstain (emit no finding) rather than firing or passing — and the rubric should note this abstain is observable in coverage.
findings.finding: The plan specifies the rubric instructs the agent to call `show_ticket(<parent_id>)` and compare scope, but does not specify what the agent should do when the parent ticket is unreadable. The `show_ticket` tool in `pai_tools.py:203-204` returns an error string (not raises) on failure, so the agent loop won't crash — but without explicit rubric guidance the agent may either fire a spurious finding (false positive) or silently pass (false negative) when the parent is unavailable (deleted ticket, permissions, store error).
findings.location: What section — CONTEXT ASSEMBLY paragraph / G7 rubric specification

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit fallback to the G7 rubric specification: if `show_ticket(parent_id)` fails or returns an empty plan, the agent must ABSTAIN-with-coverage (PASS not-applicable) rather than fire a finding — mirroring the ABSTAIN language in G1G2.md and E4.
findings.finding: No fallback is specified for the G7 agent's two-hop `show_ticket` read when the parent ticket is unavailable. The rubric instructs the agent to call `show_ticket(<own_ticket_id>)` to get `parent_id`, then `show_ticket(parent_id)` to get the parent plan. The plan says G7 gets `show_ticket` 'always' via pai_tools.py — but it says nothing about what the agent should do if `show_ticket(parent_id)` returns an error or the parent has no plan text. Every existing agentic rubric (G1G2.md, E4, G6.md) handles tool-call failures via ABSTAIN-with-coverage language. G7's rubric spec is silent on this failure mode.
findings.location: What section: 'CONTEXT ASSEMBLY'; Acceptance Criteria #4: 'G7's rubric instructs the AGENT to read the parent ... via its show_ticket tool'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py` to the Scope and update `_validate_routing_entry` (lines 131-213) to validate `require_parent_id` as an optional boolean within `applies_at`, consistent with how `scope` is validated.
findings.finding: The plan says 'validate_packaged_routing() and the applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis' but the actual applies_at schema validator is `_validate_routing_entry` in `src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py:131-213` — a file not listed in the Scope. The current validator (lines 190-212) checks only `scope`, `levels`, and `container_only` within `applies_at` and does NOT reject unknown keys — so a malformed `require_parent_id` value (e.g., a string instead of bool, or a typo key) would silently pass the CI parity gate. The plan's intent that the validator 'must accept the new axis' implies it should also VALIDATE it, but without updating overlay.py this cannot happen.
findings.location: Scope section (registry.py listed) vs actual validator location (overlay.py:131-213)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the exact type (boolean `true`) and `_validate_routing_entry` acceptance rule for `require_parent_id` in the applies_at schema — specify the error message for a non-boolean value so the validator extension and its test can be written unambiguously.
findings.finding: The value type and validation rule for the new `require_parent_id` applies_at axis are never defined. The plan says 'validate_packaged_routing() and the applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis' but does not specify: (a) what JSON type the value carries (a boolean `true`?), (b) what `_validate_routing_entry` (overlay.py:131-212) should do when it encounters the key — the function currently validates `scope`, `suppress_types`, and `suppress_when` and rejects legacy keys, but has no handling for unknown keys, (c) what error message to emit for a non-boolean value. An implementer cannot write the validator extension or the test for it without this specification.
findings.location: What section / Scope section: 'add a new boolean axis require_parent_id to the applies_at schema + applies()'; Acceptance Criteria #1

findings.finding: The plan's scope omits `src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py` (`_validate_routing_entry`), which is the SHARED schema validator that checks `applies_at` entries. Adding `require_parent_id` to the routing JSON will silently pass through `_validate_routing_entry` unvalidated (the function only checks `scope`, `suppress_types`, `suppress_when`, and rejects the two legacy keys — unknown keys are not rejected). The plan's AC states 'validate_packaged_routing()==[]' and 'the applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis', but the applies_at schema validator lives in the shared layer (overlay.py), which is not in the Scope section. Without updating `_validate_routing_entry`, a mis-wired `require_parent_id` value (e.g. a non-boolean) will never be caught by the CI parity gate, undermining the 'accepts the new axis' AC.
findings.location: Scope section; AC line 'validate_packaged_routing()==[]'; src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py:_validate_routing_entry (lines 131-212)
findings.suggested_fix: In `_validate_routing_entry` (overlay.py), after the existing `scope` validation block, add: `if 'require_parent_id' in ap and not isinstance(ap['require_parent_id'], bool): raise CriteriaError(...)`. Also add `overlay.py` to the Scope list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly scope the `has_parent_id` wiring: add `has_parent_id: bool` as a named property to `PlanContext` (derived from `bool(self.state.get('parent_id'))`), extend `applies()` to accept a `has_parent_id` kwarg, and update `route_criteria()` to pass it — these are three distinct code changes, not trivial threading.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `PlanContext` 'already knows the parent chain' and that `has_parent_id` can be trivially threaded through `route_criteria()`, but `PlanContext` (det_floor.py:95-133) has no `has_parent_id` property — `parent_id` is only accessible via the generic `ctx.state.get('parent_id')` dict lookup. Furthermore, `route_criteria()` (orchestrator.py:337-375) currently calls `registry.applies()` with only `has_children`, `ticket_type`, and `plan` — no `has_parent_id` argument. The plan frames these as configuration-level additions but they are actually new code on both PlanContext and the applies()/route_criteria() call chain.
findings.location: Routing section: 'thread the ticket's `has_parent_id` fact through `route_criteria()` from the assembled PlanContext (which already knows the parent chain)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: 'Add `has_parent_id` property to `PlanContext` (det_floor.py) derived from `bool(self.state.get("parent_id"))`, extend `applies()` in registry.py to accept `has_parent_id: bool = False`, and update the `route_criteria()` call site in orchestrator.py to pass `has_parent_id=ctx.has_parent_id`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether `has_parent_id` is a new `@property` on `PlanContext` (matching `has_children`) or sourced inline from `ctx.state.get('parent_id')` in `route_criteria()`, so the implementation surface is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The plan is silent on whether `has_parent_id` should be a new property on `PlanContext` or read inline from `ctx.state.get('parent_id')` inside `route_criteria()`. `PlanContext` currently has no `has_parent_id` field (only `has_children` as a property). `route_criteria()` receives `ctx: PlanContext` and would need to either call `bool(ctx.state.get('parent_id'))` inline, or delegate to a new PlanContext property. The plan says 'thread the ticket's has_parent_id fact through route_criteria() ... from the assembled PlanContext (which already knows the parent chain)' but does not specify the mechanism, leaving the implementer to guess the surface.
findings.location: Scope section: MECHANISM paragraph; orchestrator.py:route_criteria()
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `has_parent_id` property to PlanContext (mirroring `has_children`): `@property def has_parent_id(self) -> bool: return bool(self.state.get('parent_id'))`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC3 (or add a sub-bullet) to require that the parent-realign move is registered in passes.py MOVE_REGISTRY, verifiable by grepping passes.py for the move id or by a unit test.
findings.finding: The Scope section describes 'the coach MOVE_REGISTRY (one parent-realign move in passes.py)' as a deliverable, but AC3 only checks the *content* of the coaching move (parent-wins rule, remediation direction) without verifying the move is actually registered in passes.py — the file-change itself has no existence check.
findings.location: Scope section (passes.py move registry bullet) vs. AC3

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact placement of `has_parent_id`: either add a `has_parent_id: bool` computed property to `PlanContext` (mirroring `has_children`), or explicitly say to read `ctx.state.get('parent_id')` inline in `route_criteria()` — so the implementer and the test both target the same seam.
findings.finding: The source of the `has_parent_id` fact for `route_criteria()` / `applies()` is not specified. The plan says 'thread the ticket's has_parent_id fact through route_criteria() (orchestrator.py:337-375, which calls registry.applies()) from the assembled PlanContext.' But `PlanContext` (det_floor.py:95-133) has no `has_parent_id` property — it exposes `state: dict[str, Any]` (which stores `parent_id`) and a computed `has_children` property. The plan does not say whether to (a) add a `has_parent_id: bool` property to `PlanContext`, (b) read `ctx.state.get('parent_id')` directly inside `route_criteria()`, or (c) add a `has_parent_id` parameter to `applies()`. Each choice has different test implications.
findings.location: What section: 'thread the ticket's has_parent_id fact through route_criteria()'; Scope section: 'orchestrator.py (route_criteria() threads the ticket's has_parent_id fact into applies())'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `has_parent_id` property to `PlanContext` (analogous to `has_children`): `@property\ndef has_parent_id(self) -> bool: return bool(self.state.get('parent_id'))`. Then pass `has_parent_id=ctx.has_parent_id` to `registry.applies()` in `route_criteria()`. This mirrors the existing pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `require_parent_id` justifies a new `applies_at` schema axis (Rule-of-Three: only G7 uses it); evaluate the simpler alternative of a `suppress_when: ["parentless_leaf"]` condition or letting G7's agent self-gate via PASS not-applicable when no `parent_id` is found.
findings.finding: The new `require_parent_id` boolean axis on `applies_at` — plus the corresponding new `has_parent_id` parameter threaded through `applies()` and `route_criteria()` — has exactly one current consumer (G7). The existing `suppress_when` mechanism (registry.py:334-338) already supports named suppression conditions, and could express this with a new `"parentless_leaf"` condition (zero new schema axis, one extra branch in the existing handler). Alternatively, G7 could self-gate as PASS not-applicable when no parent_id is found, consistent with how LLM-routed overlays already self-filter (registry.py:369). A new schema axis with one consumer is premature per Rule-of-Three.
findings.location: What section — 'ROUTING … add a new boolean axis `require_parent_id` to the `applies_at` schema and to `applies()`'; Scope — 'src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py … add the `require_parent_id` axis to the applies_at schema + `applies()`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"parentless_leaf"` to the existing `suppress_when` handler in `registry.applies()` (one new `elif` branch mirroring the `mechanical_leaf` branch at registry.py:337-338), set G7's routing to `suppress_when: ["parentless_leaf"]`, and define `is_parentless_leaf(plan, state)` reading `state.get("parent_id")`. No new schema axis, no new function parameter, no orchestrator threading change.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md is regenerated and contains a G7 entry (e.g. grep for 'G7' or 'parent-containment' in the file as a CI content check).
findings.finding: The Scope section lists 'the regenerated docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md' as a deliverable, but no Acceptance Criterion covers its existence or content — it is an uncovered described deliverable.
findings.location: Scope section ('the regenerated docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md') vs. Acceptance Criteria (AC1–AC8)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly scope the regenerated `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md` to include the new `require_parent_id` routing axis (purpose, valid values, example entry) so future criterion authors can discover and reuse it; alternatively, add a CLAUDE.md note in the plan-review package about the extended applies_at schema.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new `require_parent_id` boolean axis on the `applies_at` routing schema — a new authoring contract for criterion developers — but does not explicitly scope the regenerated `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md` to document this axis, nor mention a CLAUDE.md update. Without explicit coverage, future criterion authors will be unaware the axis exists and cannot reuse it.
findings.location: Scope section: 'src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py … add the `require_parent_id` axis to the applies_at schema' and 'the regenerated docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, amend the guide-regeneration bullet to read: 'regenerated docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md — must include a section documenting the `require_parent_id` axis: when to use it, its interaction with `scope`, and an example routing entry.' No ADR is needed for this narrow schema extension if the guide covers it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove or correct the `completion.py:18` citation: the file does not exist in the repo; replace with the verifiable source (`pai_tools.py:rebar_tools()` which always includes `show_ticket`).
findings.finding: The plan cites `completion.py:18` as evidence that the completion verifier reads tickets the same way as G7's agent will. No `completion.py` file exists anywhere in the repository — it is only referenced in code comments (config.py:40, operations.py:31, gate_ops.py:19). The line citation is unverifiable. While the underlying claim (show_ticket is available to all AGENT criteria) is independently verifiable via pai_tools.py, the specific citation is a dangling reference.
findings.location: Context Assembly section: 'the completion verifier reads tickets the same way, completion.py:18'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'the completion verifier reads tickets the same way, completion.py:18' with 'the same show_ticket tool available to all AGENT criteria (pai_tools.py:rebar_tools(), which always includes show_ticket regardless of criterion id).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Evaluate the rubric-internal applicability self-gate (agent calls `show_ticket(own_id)`, checks `parent_id`, passes `not-applicable` if absent) as an alternative to the `require_parent_id` schema axis, and document why schema extension is preferable if that is the conclusion.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new `require_parent_id` boolean axis on the `applies_at` routing schema — a schema-extension approach with no precedent in the existing criteria system — without evaluating the established alternative: having the AGENT rubric handle its own applicability internally (like G6 and T8 do, passing 'not-applicable' when their trigger condition is absent). Multiple AGENT-tier criteria (G6, T8) already self-gate on a runtime condition detected by the model; the plan offers no justification for why a new schema axis is preferable to the rubric-level self-gate, which would require zero schema changes and zero orchestrator threading.
findings.location: What section: 'ROUTING — fires ONLY on leaf tickets WITH a parent' / 'add a new boolean axis `require_parent_id`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a section to the plan comparing the two approaches: (a) schema axis `require_parent_id` + orchestrator threading vs. (b) rubric-level self-gate (G6/T8 pattern). If the schema axis is chosen, state the concrete reason (e.g., 'self-gate would still run the agent loop and incur ~85× cost even for parentless tickets, whereas the routing axis filters pre-LLM at near-zero cost').

findings.finding: The plan places G7 in `CODEBASE_GROUNDED` alongside E4/G1G2/A1/G6, but `CODEBASE_GROUNDED` is explicitly defined in the codebase as criteria 'whose job is to reason about the live codebase.' G7 reasons about the ticket store (via `show_ticket`) — not the codebase — exactly like G3 and G4, which are in `AGENT_TIER` but NOT in `CODEBASE_GROUNDED`. Placing G7 in `CODEBASE_GROUNDED` is an unverified (and likely incorrect) capability assertion that could misroute Pass-2 verification or agentic tooling decisions that key on `CODEBASE_GROUNDED`.
findings.location: What section — 'register G7 in CANONICAL_LLM/AGENT_TIER/CODEBASE_GROUNDED'; registry.py:66
findings.suggested_fix: Register G7 in `CANONICAL_LLM` and `AGENT_TIER` only (mirroring G3/G4's placement), not in `CODEBASE_GROUNDED`. Update the Scope section accordingly.

findings.finding: AC4's in-session proof is explicitly bounded to file existence ('CI-verifiable that the file exists'), but the AC simultaneously requires specific content — the 'exact query,' the '0.6–0.7 promotion band + trigger,' and 'where the decision is recorded' — with no proving command that verifies any of that content; an executor can satisfy the AC with a near-empty placeholder file.
findings.location: AC4 — 'In-session proof (a COMMITTED repo file, CI-verifiable that the file exists...) documents (a) the exact query... (b) the promotion trigger... (c) where the actual promotion decision will be recorded'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC4: 'Verifiable via `grep -cE "REVIEW_RESULT|fire.rate|promotion" <path>` returning ≥ 3 distinct matches, AND the doc contains a runnable jq/SQL snippet (identifiable by a code fence) that reads from REVIEW_RESULT sidecars.' This keeps the bar in-session and objective without requiring post-deployment data.

findings.finding: The documentation file location is specified as an unresolved OR ('docs/plan-review-gate.md or docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/g7-promotion-path.md'), leaving the executor to choose the path rather than acting on a stated default.
findings.location: What section — 'POSTURE: ship default_posture: advisory...' paragraph; AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the OR with a single path: `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/g7-promotion-path.md` (the new file avoids editing a shared doc and is easier to CI-anchor with `test -f`).

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
